triggers.name: Aviat: Interface [SFP1]: High Light Level
triggers.description: Triggers when the average received light level is above {$SFP_HIGH_LIGHT} dBm. Recovers when the average light level is below {$SFP_HIGH_LIGHT} dBm. Verify against current SFP module spec sheet.

triggers.name: Aviat: Interface [SFP1]: Low Light Level
triggers.description: Triggers when the average received light level is below {$SFP_LOW_LIGHT} dBm. Recovers when the average light level is above {$SFP_LOW_LIGHT} dBm. Verify against current SFP module spec sheet.

triggers.name: Aviat: Interface [SFP2]: High Light Level
triggers.description: Triggers when the average received light level is above {$SFP_HIGH_LIGHT} dBm. Recovers when the average light level is below {$SFP_HIGH_LIGHT} dBm. Verify against current SFP module spec sheet.

triggers.name: Aviat: Interface [SFP2]: Low Light Level
triggers.description: Triggers when the average received light level is below {$SFP_LOW_LIGHT} dBm. Recovers when the average light level is above {$SFP_LOW_LIGHT} dBm. Verify against current SFP module spec sheet.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Inbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of inbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system,
and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Inbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of inbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of inbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Unknown Packet Discards
item_prototypes.description: For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of packets received via the interface which were discarded because of an unknown or unsupported protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces that support protocol multiplexing the number of transmission units received via the interface which were discarded because of an unknown or unsupported protocol.  For any interface that does not support protocol multiplexing, this counter will always be 0.  Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Bits received
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets received on the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifInOctets. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Outbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of outbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system,
and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Outbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of outbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of outbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}{#IFDESCR}): Bits sent
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifOutOctets.Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Operational status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interface is ready to transmit and receive network traffic
- It should change todormant(5) if the interface is waiting for external actions (such as a serial line waiting for an incoming connection)
- It should remain in the down(2) state if and only if there is a fault that prevents it from going to the up(1) state
- It should remain in the notPresent(6) state if the interface has missing(typically, hardware) components.

trigger_prototypes.expression: {$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1 and last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2 and (last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1)<>last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2))
trigger_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Link down
trigger_prototypes.description:
This trigger expression works as follows:
1. It can be triggered if the operations status is down.
2. `{$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1` - a user can redefine context macro to value - 0. That marks this interface as not important. No new trigger will be fired if this interface is down.
3. `{TEMPLATE_NAME:METRIC.diff()}=1` - the trigger fires only if the operational status was up to (1) sometime before (so, do not fire for the 'eternal off' interfaces.)

WARNING: if closed manually - it will not fire again on the next poll, because of .diff.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Interface Type
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interface is ready to transmit and receive network traffic
- It should change todormant(5) if the interface is waiting for external actions (such as a serial line waiting for an incoming connection)
- It should remain in the down(2) state if and only if there is a fault that prevents it from going to the up(1) state
- It should remain in the notPresent(6) state if the interface has missing(typically, hardware) components.

trigger_prototypes.expression:
change(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])<0 and last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])>0
and (
last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=6 or
last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=7 or
last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=11 or
last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=62 or
last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=69 or
last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=117
)
and
(last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])<>2)
trigger_prototypes.recovery_expression:
(change(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])>0 and last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2)>0) or
(last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2)
trigger_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Ethernet has changed to lower speed than it was before
trigger_prototypes.description: This Ethernet connection has transitioned down from its known maximum speed. This might be a sign of autonegotiation issues. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

dependencies.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Link down
dependencies.expression: {$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1 and last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2 and (last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1)<>last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2))

trigger_prototypes.expression:
(avg(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.in[ifHCInOctets.{#SNMPINDEX}],15m)>({$IF.UTIL.MAX:"{#IFNAME}"}/100)*last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}]) or
avg(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.out[ifHCOutOctets.{#SNMPINDEX}],15m)>({$IF.UTIL.MAX:"{#IFNAME}"}/100)*last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])) and
last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])>0
trigger_prototypes.recovery_expression:
avg(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.in[ifHCInOctets.{#SNMPINDEX}],15m)<(({$IF.UTIL.MAX:"{#IFNAME}"}-3)/100)*last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}]) and
avg(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.out[ifHCOutOctets.{#SNMPINDEX}],15m)<(({$IF.UTIL.MAX:"{#IFNAME}"}-3)/100)*last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])
trigger_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): High bandwidth usage
trigger_prototypes.description: The utilization of the network interface is close to its estimated maximum bandwidth.

dependencies.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Link down
dependencies.expression: {$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1 and last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2 and (last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1)<>last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2))

trigger_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): High error rate
trigger_prototypes.description: It recovers when it is below 80% of the `{$IF.ERRORS.WARN:"{#IFNAME}"}` threshold.

dependencies.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFDESCR}): Link down
dependencies.expression: {$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1 and last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2 and (last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1)<>last(/.Aviat by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2))

trigger_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}: RSL High
trigger_prototypes.description: RSL is abnormally high (>{$RSL.HIGH} dBm). May indicate near-end reflections, interference, or misconfiguration. Verify against link budget.

trigger_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}: RSL Low
trigger_prototypes.description: RSL is below acceptable threshold (<{$RSL.LOW} dBm). Link may be affected by rain fade, obstruction, or equipment issues.

macros.description: Filter out loopbacks, nulls, docker veth links and docker0 bridge by default
Minimum overload threshold for SFP modules
Maximum stressed sensitivity for SFP modules
RSL high threshold (dBm) — abnormally strong signal, may indicate near-end reflections or interference
RSL low threshold (dBm) — signal degradation warning, link at risk
